title: Though O Lord, Art God Alone

verses.text: Thou, O Lord, art God alone, everlasting is thy throne; through the ages men shall sing praise to heav'n's eternal King. Thou, enthroned above the skies, wilt for Zion's help arise; let thy grace to her appear, for the promised time is near.

If with love compassionate we, thy servants, mourn her state, wilt not thou, O gracious Lord, help in Zion's need afford? Lord, thy glory shall appear, kings and nations then shall fear; and thy name shall be adored when thy Zion is restored.

This all ages shall record for the glory of the Lord; thou dost hear the humble prayer, for the helpless thou dost care. Thou eternal art, and great, heav'n and earth thou didst create; heav'n and earth shall pass away, changeless thou shall live for aye.

As one lays a garment by, thou wilt change the starry sky like a vesture worn and old; but thy years shall ne'er be told. Thou wilt make thy servants' race ever live before thy face, and forever at thy side children's children shall abide.
